item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why the "Nobody" bit doe.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image appears to be from here, which forbids commercial usage. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. Honestly I'd like to see an extended experiment log for this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did not know that. I usually just browse by clicking 'random'. Perhaps linking the SCP's pages togethr?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should probably note that this item, among others, relates to a series of tales and SCPs that tell something of a shared story, the so-called "Breath of the World" series. Reading them may shed some more information on what this is all about. The list of the items pertaining to this series can be found in my author page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The item is interesting, but I dislike your use of vague symbolism. The image doesn't really give much information, and the bit about Nobody delivering the item feel tacked on. I feel the acquisition process or previous owner's use could have given us more information.

That said, I like the item overall. I wouldn't be able to criticize it like this if it wasn't rich with mystery and written with such a depth that I could actually dislike the symbolism.

Upvote, but I'd like to see what your actual theory is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I like it a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing about the aspects is, gender works rather strangely with them. Themis is indeed female, but the entity the gem of aspects originally belonged to is male, hence the pronoun. Regardless, there is no real connection to the actual goddess (or rather titaness) here, it is for all intents and purposes a nickname of sort.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um… n00b question, but is the Themis mentioned by the Mouth in the third log supposed to correlate to the figure from Greek mythology? I thought so at first, but the next bit refers used the pronoun "he", and as far as I'm aware the Themis of myths is a goddess? Or is it just some guy with a fancy name/codename?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the link; I was a great story. I hope that's what Nobody turns into, and that it brings interest in him and the Breath.

item.title: Alexylva product?
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea and it reminded me about the Crystal Skull from Alexylva university GoI. Still no clues about the meaning of the picture, though…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, you could always read the tale involving the Breath (linked in a comment below).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thirded, i want more. morrrre

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good opinion to have, and it's at least partly true. Since I'm still writing stuff about the Breath (like in the second part of favors), I don't won't to give away any more than that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To me, they seem to be separate aspects, or avatars, of a single entity, the way Catholics have the Father, Son and Holy Spirit, and the Voice of God, etc. the Heart, Mind, Breath, etc may seem different but be part of the same. Just my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
different results occurred even when using the same subject.

This is the only problem I had with this, retesting the same subject allows for cross-contaminating different tests. Sorta like cooking two pies without cleaning the pan between consecutive servings, bits of the old pie get into the new one. (yes I know I am being nit-picky)

Other than that I loved it, an SCP that not only is interesting, but also makes me want to know more about it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When you put it like that, I guess you have a point. That's the thing about patterns and symbolism- they don't always translate well. I really don't won't to spoil people's ideas with my idea about the entire thing- the mystery is supposed to be a part of the articles appeal.
I'll just say the gem is a means of communication-it doesn't hold anything in itself. Oh, and Zeus and Jupiter are simply different names (Greek and Roman) for the same god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah… that's about as easy to figure out as "What am I thinking about right now?" I gotta be sceptical there's any real clues here, and not just random association. But the SCP itself is nicely written.

As an example, just going by the number thing, there are four men, the gordian knots branches into three lesser knots, with a line drawn from it into a wheel with four spokes, the wheel itself is circular that could be a zero, the pedestal could be seen as a 1 which gives you ten and this is why hiding pattern in numbers and things doesn't really work. Hell even that there was a chariot isn't noticeable unless you're told about it already. I guess the knot anchors the chariot so that when it is cut Zeus can take Jupiter on a sweet ride or something. Then they all fall apart into different body parts and decides to hang out in a gem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are forgetting the chariot the man is standing next to. And that small figure? That's the figure of a god (Jupiter, or Zeus). Another thing worth noticing is the strand from the knot going straight into the chariot and then into the ground itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say, I was a little worried coming in to the description that it was simply another semi-oracular item, but the conversations held with the SCP were what really sold me. I thought it was excellent work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's good, but Nobody/The Breath feels tacked on, really extraneous to the item itself.
Possibly this is because I still don't get what you are hinting at above. What am I looking for in the picture? I see four figures, a man brandishing a sword (presumably Alexander), a second man, a woman, and either a midget or a child standing on a pedestal. I'm not picking up on anything that relates to either body parts or the personalities/functions that seem to go with them. Unless you are saying that there is signifigance in the mere fact that there are 4 figures and four aspects not counting breath. But that seems really insubstantial and leads to no interesting implications.

Second, -1 is objectively four different personalities. They refer to themselves by separate names and each has a consitent behavior pattern that is markedly distinct from the others. Wether these personalities are part of a single entity (the ghostly equivalent of multiple personality disorder) or are 4 separate things that happen to time-share a magic walkie-talkie is irrelevant. The fact that the research team has formed competing schools of opinion on the matter has no place in what is ostensibly a formal briefing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't mean something outrageous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Favors-Part One.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where was the breath mentioned before? Please either give a link or say the name and what it is (i.e. tale…).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A little hint: take a good look at the picture itself- what number is significant there? How does this number show in the article, and what is the notable exception to it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm…

Wild guess time
-

I am guessing the Gordian Knot somehow held these entities in check, and once split it let them loose again. This gem is a new way to hold them together?


Right or wrong, I rather like the intriguingness (I know that's not a word, but it is now) of this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I guess I was. Now that I've seen a few people I very much respect upvote (Souless, Dokotri, Drewbear, Noah, you), I can relax a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, I told you it was good. You were all worried over nothing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. Good to see more of Nobody and the Breath.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nobody blends in. It's not like he'd wear a gray suit and a phrygian cap, a viking helmet, or a pair of fuzzy antennae.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Try to look at the picture itself: there is a something about the knot and the scene itself that is reflected in the entities (or maybe aspects of the same entity) who communicate through it- all but the last one, which is different from the first four.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. I like it. (is it bad that what pushed my up vote was that it confirmed the fedora, rather than another hat?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, puzzling, yet coherent. I would suggest having a footnote for Themis and the Maenads. Most people wouldn't know them on command, at least, I certainly didn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. A nice, somewhat puzzling SCP. I like this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one intrigues me; I'm still trying to decipher the connection to the knot though.
However, the writeup seems a little weak in a way that I can't put my finger on. It's possible this feeling is due to not fully understanding the concept behind the object, so I'm wondering if anyone else feels the same way.

I would love to upvote this, but something's missing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That one is a bit connected to one of my tales. Maybe I shouldn't have added it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the last Addendum, the rest of it seems okay to me.
